basics.name: Michael Klein
basics.label: Cloud Platform Architect/Engineer
basics.summary: Cloud Architect and Engineer with 20+ years across Silicon Valley and Europe, scaling Google Cloud Platform (GCP) from zero to €4M+ p.a. while cutting spend ~50% vs. on-prem. Hands-on with GCP, Platform Engineering, and CloudOps.

work.name: EPO
work.summary: Lead architect for the European Patent Office's Google Cloud adoption — foundation, team build-out, infrastructure automation, CloudOps, and FinOps.
highlights: Drove GCP adoption from 0 to > €4M p.a., saving ~50% compared to on-premises
As Cloud Architect, drive cost efficiency: ~40% effective discount on GCP list price through architectural design, custom pricing, and committed-use agreements (paying ~60 cents per €1 of list)
Designed and built two Enterprise-grade GCP organizations incl. their foundational setup as well as the operational processes and boundaries
Directly involved in the implementation of several GCP discount agreements, e.g., with ~€270K additional saving in 2023 and ~€350K in 2024
Helped to increase our overall 2024 production output by 2% (~€50M)
Built the core GCP team and grew it to 6 FTEs, including the hiring of 3 cloud architects and engineers
Key player in introducing Enterprise-grade Kubernetes "the hard way", individually contributing to cluster- and application-level Observability
As Architect, Common Build Services, reduced CI capacity by ~20% while sustaining ~800 nightly builds and thousands of builds per day
Sole developer of `cecli`, the missing `gcloud browsers` command

work.name: IDS Scheer (later acquired by Software AG, now SAG ARIS GmbH)
work.summary: Development and team management roles for ARIS product and ARIS Web Development.
highlights: As Development Manager for Oracle BPA Suite (an ARIS OEM product), achieved ~140% ROI in 3 years
Built up an office and a development outpost for 5 FTEs abroad, in the US
As Team Manager Web Development, recovered a 7-figure deal at risk of customer churn
Mentored a thesis

work.name: Insiders Technologies
work.summary: Software engineer working on web-based information retrieval and search systems.
highlights: Within the first 6 weeks, developed a working prototype of a web-based search system, demoed at the world's largest IT trade fair, CeBIT

volunteer.summary: I served as a volunteer firefighter for over 30 years, starting as a junior cadet and eventually becoming a state-certified incident commander and group leader. During my tenure, I participated in over 100 firefighting operations, contributing to the safety and well-being of the community. Additionally, I created and developed the organization's website, enhancing communication and outreach efforts.
highlights: Became a state-certified incident commander and group leader
Participated in 100+ firefighting operations
Created and developed the organization's website between 1998 and 2006

courses: Software Design and Implementation Using C++

certificates.name: Google Cloud Certified Gen AI Leader
Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Developer
Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect
Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

publications.name: The Sovereign Cloud Fail-Over Pattern
publications.summary: Failure happens: Fail over or recover to the Sovereign Part of a Cloud Platform. A Cloud Infrastructure Design Pattern.

publications.name: Geofencing in Flutter
publications.summary: What Geofence limitations exist and how to work around them in Flutter.

publications.name: Dynamic (and Secure) Google Maps API Key in Flutter
publications.summary: How not to hard-code and distribute a Google Maps API Key in your App.

projects.name: Wyatt
projects.description: A location- and time-based reminder app for Android and iOS.
highlights: Implemented the app using Flutter, enabling cross-platform development
Integrated Geofencing and Google Maps
Published "Dynamic (and Secure) Google Maps API Key in Flutter" on Medium, covering API-key handling pitfalls in Flutter applications
Published "Geofencing in Flutter" on Medium, detailing the implementation and use cases of Geofencing in the app

projects.name: Agility
projects.description: A cloud-native implementation of how to measure agility using Kubernetes Deployment Frequency.
highlights: Implemented this project to illustrate the Deployment Frequency (DF) KPI from Forsgren, Humble, and Kim's "Accelerate"
Built it using Golang and Kubernetes, demonstrating how to measure agility through Kubernetes Deployment Frequency
Integrated Prometheus and Grafana for monitoring and visualization of deployment metrics

quotes.text: Whatever tomorrow brings, I'll be there. With open arms and open eyes.
quotes.author: Incubus, "Drive"

quotes.text: The only winning move is not to play.
quotes.author: Joshua, "WarGames"

faqs.question: Can you send me your CV/resume?
What do you expect from a recruiter?
faqs.answer: Read. The. Profile. (Let's call it RTFP.) Any questions? Please ask.

faqs.question: Do you hate recruiters?
faqs.answer: Absolutely not. However, I have a hard time when positions are to be filled with the right people and I'm obviously not. Again, RTFP. Thanks!
